pattern: Every sentence is single-space-separated words with no stray leading, trailing, or doubled spaces, so a word count is just one more than the number of spaces -- take the largest over all sentences.
hints: Since spacing is guaranteed clean (single spaces, no leading/trailing), you don't need a general tokenizer -- counting spaces is enough.
The number of words in a sentence is exactly (number of spaces) + 1.
Track a running maximum as you go through the list of sentences.

edge_inputs.description: single sentence with no spaces at all, one word
one sentence clearly has the most words
a tie for the maximum word count among several sentences
a long single word beats a shorter multi-word sentence in length but not word count
sentence built entirely of many one-letter words up to the length cap
input: a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a a

edge_inputs.description: all sentences have exactly one word

stress.description: random sentence lengths (in words) built from single-letter tokens joined by single spaces
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    sentences = []
    for _ in range(n):
        word_count = rng.randint(1, 50)
        words = []
        total_len = 0
        for _ in range(word_count):
            wl = rng.randint(1, 3)
            if total_len + wl + (1 if words else 0) > 100:
                break
            words.append(''.join(rng.choice('abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz') for _ in range(wl)))
            total_len += wl + (1 if len(words) > 1 else 0)
        sentences.append(' '.join(words))
    return [sentences]
